name: bua
description: AI-powered browser automation for Go — describe tasks in plain English, let the agent handle the clicks.

commit.message: Update default model in E2E tests to `gemini-3-flash-preview`
Add `ScreenshotDir` configuration to enable saving screenshots during E2E tests
Update `.gitignore` to include `.screenshots/` directory
Enable annotations and highlights in E2E test agent configuration
Refactor `ShowHighlight` configuration to simplify handling and remove pointer usage

Add comprehensive README documentation for BUA library

- Introduced an extensive README covering installation, configuration, features, use cases, examples, and architecture.
- Highlighted key functionalities, including natural language automation, vision-enabled workflows, stealth mode, and tab management.
- Provided code snippets for quick start, advanced setups, and debugging.
- Included a detailed comparison with similar Python-based solutions for browser automation.
- Ensured clarity around prerequisites, supported tools, and contributing guidelines.

Add annotation functionality for interactive elements in screenshots

- Implemented annotation logic to add bounding boxes and labels to screenshots.
- Introduced `AnnotationConfig` for customizable appearance (colors, borders, labels, etc.).
- Added `CaptureWithAnnotations` and related methods in the `screenshot` package.
- Integrated annotation support into the browser, agent, and examples modules.
- Updated examples to demonstrate annotated screenshots for debugging and LLM usage.

Enhance screenshot workflow with page readiness, content validation, and stability checks

- Introduced options for detecting blank pages (`ErrBlankPage`) and validating screenshot content (`ErrEmptyScreenshot`).
- Added functions for page readiness checks (`waitForPageReady`, `IsPageReady`) and content verification (`isEmptyImage`).
- Refactored screenshot logic to incorporate customizable options, stability waits, and content validation.
- Added new methods for capturing screenshots after actions and for safe LLM usage (`ForLLMSafe`, `CaptureAfterAction`).
- Updated browser and agent modules to utilize enhanced screenshot logic with better error handling and blank page skips.

Add screenshot capturing support and enhance multimodal interaction

- Introduced `ScreenshotDir` in configuration to enable saving screenshots during browser automation.
- Updated steps to include `ScreenshotPath` for better debugging and result traceability.
- Implemented additional logic to capture and save screenshots at various stages of the automation flow.
- Enhanced model interaction with multimodal content, combining text and images where vision support is enabled.
- Revised existing example to utilize the new screenshot functionality and folder configuration.

Add comprehensive end-to-end testing framework with task-based YAML structure

- Introduced a modular E2E framework for browser agent validation, supporting YAML-based test definition (`basic.yaml`, `forms.yaml`, `scraping.yaml`, and `scroll.yaml`).
- Implemented core test runner `run_tests.go` with options for filtering by category, individual test name, and debug configurations.
- Created tests for navigation, form filling, scraping, and scrolling, covering key browser automation scenarios.
- Added `README.md` detailing framework usage, YAML structure, debugging tips, and CI/CD integration.
- Ensured compatibility with `bua-go` agent configurations and Gemini models.

Introduce stealth mode for anti-detection in browser automation

- Added `StealthConfig` struct to configure stealth features like UserAgent spoofing, WebGL vendor spoofing, timezone overrides, locale setting, and human-like delays.
- Implemented `applyStealthMode` to inject anti-detection scripts, with additional support for WebGL overrides and random delays for human-like behavior.
- Injected stealth scripts during browser initialization and new tab creation when enabled.
- Enhanced mouse and keyboard interactions with human-like offsets, linear movements, and randomized delays.
- Provided default stealth settings via `DefaultStealthConfig` and `GetStealthLaunchFlags` for consistency.
- Updated browser launch process to include stealth-specific Chrome flags.
- Applied stealth mode to all navigations and interactions for better bot detection evasion.
Update quick start example to use DuckDuckGo for search queries
refactor
Remove all outdated example files under `examples` directory.

Enhance action highlighting with direct element flashing, improved animations, and streamlined API

- Refactor highlight logic for more reliable and visually clear feedback by adopting a "browser-use" style approach.
- Implement direct element styling with orange flashing outlines, smoother animations, and better crosshair accuracy.
- Add new support for action labels, scroll indicators, and improved label positioning.
- Deprecate corner bracket-based highlights in favor of flashing outlines for better UX.
- Update `highlight_demo` example to reflect new features, improve descriptions, and align with updated methods.
- Ensure backward compatibility for coordinate-based clicking and typing highlights.

Update `highlight_demo` example with `EnhancedDOM`, `ShowAnnotations`, and `PresetQuality`

- Switched model to `Gemini3Pro` for improved performance.
- Enabled `EnhancedDOM` and `ShowAnnotations` for richer interactivity and debugging.
- Updated `Preset` to `Quality` for better demo visualization.

Add enhanced DOM extraction with advanced filtering and interactivity detection

- Introduced CDP-based enhanced DOM extraction, supporting features like paint order filtering, containment filtering, and cursor-based interactive element detection.
- Added support for tracking new elements, merging accessibility properties, and backend node ID mapping for stable references.
- Updated `bua.Config` to include `EnhancedDOM` with default value `false` and enhanced fallback logic for error resilience.
- Integrated enhanced element map extraction in `browser` and `agent` modules for improved filtering, usability, and token efficiency.
- Documented new capabilities in `README.md` with examples and feature highlights.

Streamline `README.md` with simplified config examples, introduce presets

- Simplified configuration examples to highlight minimal requirements and preset-based usage.
- Introduced new `Preset` configurations (`Fast`, `Efficient`, `Balanced`, `Quality`, `Max`) with clear usage scenarios and trade-offs.
- Deprecated `TokenPreset` in favor of the more intuitive `Preset` field, with migration guidance provided.
- Enhanced clarity on advanced configuration options, token usage, and manual overrides.
- Added section on token counting and improved documentation for better user understanding.

Add examples for basic usage, highlight demo, and modal scrolling

- Introduced three new example files under the `examples` directory: `basic/main.go`, `highlight_demo/main.go`, and `modal_scroll/main.go`.
- Demonstrated basic functionality, action highlighting, and modal scrolling capabilities of `bua-go`.
- Enhanced examples with clear comments, configurable options, and practical scenarios for better understanding and usability.
Remove legacy E2E test framework implementation from `examples/e2e_test/main.go`.

Add `Highlighter` utility for visual feedback in browser automation

- Introduced a `Highlighter` class in `browser/highlight.go` for enhanced user feedback during automation.
- Added support for animated element highlights, coordinate indicators, scroll directions, and typing labels.
- Implemented customizable delays, styles, and enable/disable functionality for flexible use cases.
- Ensured efficient cleanup of highlights after actions.

Add E2E test framework with YAML-based test definitions and categories

- Introduced an end-to-end testing system for `bua-go` to validate browser automation capabilities.
- Added YAML-based test definitions for organized test management under categories (`basic`, `forms`, `scraping`, `scroll`).
- Implemented a test runner (`tests/e2e/run_tests.go`) with support for filtering by category or test name, verbose outputs, and headless modes.
- Documented testing philosophy, structure, and examples in `tests/e2e/README.md`.
- Designed modular tests for navigation, interactions, data scraping, and scrolling behaviors across real-world websites.

Add `Preset` configurations and deprecate `TokenPreset`

- Introduced `Preset` configurations (`Fast`, `Efficient`, `Balanced`, `Quality`, `Max`) for streamlined and consistent management of token usage, quality, and settings.
- Deprecated `TokenPreset` and related options in favor of `Preset` for simplicity and better integration.
- Updated `Config` structure to include `Preset` and enhanced documentation for ease of use.
- Refactored `applyPreset` logic to handle default values and override checks dynamically.
- Improved element and screenshot handling with `Preset`-specific optimizations.
- Enhanced structured thinking, goal tracking, and reasoning capture in agent steps.
Remove all test files from `agent`, `browser`, and `bua` modules.
Simplify and restructure `SystemPrompt` for enhanced readability and XML-style organization.

Add testing philosophy and e2e test guidelines to `CLAUDE.md`

- Documented the limitations of unit testing for AI browser agents.
- Introduced e2e testing system with usage examples and YAML-based test structure.
- Added instructions for creating and debugging new tests.
- Provided test categories overview for better organization.
Add `gopkg.in/yaml.v3` and additional dependencies to `go.mod` and `go.sum`
Remove `confidence` module and its associated tests from the `agent` package.

Improve modal scrolling and detection mechanisms

- Enhanced documentation for modal and popup scrolling in prompts.
- Added robust auto-detection handling for scrollable modals in `ScrollInput` using `auto_detect`.
- Introduced a new browser method `FindScrollableModal` for identifying scrollable containers.
- Updated `agent.go` to incorporate modal auto-detection in `scrollHandler` with detailed feedback.
- Improved `verification.go` to flag potential modals after significant DOM changes.
- Added new flags in `bua.go` for better media playback and additional anti-detection safeguards.

Add timeout-based stabilization to avoid blocking on animated/video pages

- Introduced `waitForStableWithTimeout` to prevent indefinite blocking on pages with animations or videos.
- Updated navigation and interactions to use stability checks with defined time limits.
- Improved reliability for scenarios involving continuous DOM changes (e.g., Instagram Reels).
Remove redundant JSON schema description for `ExcludeScreenshot` in `GetPageStateInput`

Add `TokenPresetTextOnly` for faster, low-token operation

- Introduced `TokenPresetTextOnly`, which disables screenshots for speed and reduced token usage (~5-15K tokens per page).
- Added `TextOnly` configuration option to skip screenshots entirely in agent operations.
- Updated element and screenshot handling to respect `TextOnly` mode across tools and browser interactions.
- Refined examples and documentation to demonstrate `TextOnly` usage for text extraction and high-speed scenarios.

Add token presets, enhanced configuration options, and efficient screenshot handling

- Introduced customizable token presets (`Efficient`, `Balanced`, `Quality`, `Maximum`) for improved token usage management.
- Added configuration options for `MaxElements`, `ScreenshotMaxWidth`, and `ScreenshotQuality`.
- Implemented compressed and resized screenshots for LLM context optimization, reducing token usage.
- Refactored element and screenshot handling to balance quality and efficiency in LLM interactions.
Update research example to use `gemini-3-pro-preview` model

Add rate-limiting handling with retry logic and configurable delays in tasks

- Implemented `parseRateLimitDelay` to interpret retry delays for 429 errors.
- Enabled automatic retries with backoff for rate-limited tasks in `Run`.
- Updated examples to add delays between tasks for better rate-limit management.
- Clarified model and configuration details in research examples.

Add configurable screenshot modes (`normal` and `smart`) to browser agent for improved action awareness

- Introduced `ScreenshotMode` with `normal` and `smart` options for screenshots in action responses.
- Added base64-encoded screenshots to action outputs (`click`, `type`, `scroll`, and `navigate`) in `smart` mode.
- Refactored `ShowAnnotations` for efficient batch processing and rendering in browser overlay.
- Improved screenshot handling to use viewport capture by default to avoid artifacts.

Add confidence tracking system to `agent` package

- Implemented `ConfidenceTracker`, `ConfidenceScore`, and related types to assess confidence levels in task execution.
- Added methods for element confidence scoring, page state difference calculation, and task confidence aggregation.
- Developed extensive unit tests and benchmarks for confidence routines in `confidence_test.go`.

Add comprehensive test coverage for `export`, `agent`, and `browser` packages

- Implemented unit tests in `export/adktool_test.go` for `BrowserTool`, `MultiBrowserTool`, and related configurations.
- Created extensive tests in `agent/agent_test.go` covering configuration, findings storage, concurrency, and logger behavior.
- Developed integration and unit tests in `browser/browser_test.go` for navigation, scrolling, tab management, screenshots, and element interactions.
- Added benchmarks for performance-critical methods across `agent` and `browser` packages.
Simplify task prompts in `examples/scraping` and `examples/simple` for improved readability and autonomy

Remove `extract` tool and integrate structured `element map` processing for autonomous data handling

- Replaced `extract` tool with a direct `element map` processing approach for improved modularity and clarity.
- Updated autonomous planning logic and system prompts to leverage `get_page_state` for structured data extraction.
- Simplified data handling in `done` tool, consolidating summary and extracted data into a single, unified structure.
- Refactored pending steps tracking and enhanced function response handling for streamlined workflows.

Remove `tools` package and refactor `prompts` for enhanced browser agent automation

- Fully removed `tools/tools.go` and related tool definitions, streamlining the codebase.
- Consolidated and updated `prompts` to reflect autonomous goal-oriented behavior, improving clarity and simplifying agent instructions.
- Reduced redundancy and enhanced modularity in system prompt logic.

Add support for scrolling within specific elements (modals, sidebars, popups)

- Updated `scroll` tool with a new `element_id` parameter to enable container-specific scrolling.
- Modified `ScrollInElement` function in `browser/browser.go` to handle element-based scrolling using JavaScript execution.
- Enhanced prompts and documentation to clarify usage of `element_id` for modal and popup scrolling.
- Adjusted agent behavior to differentiate between page-level and element-specific scroll actions.

Add token tracking, step/task timing, and enhanced logging metrics to Logger

- Introduced fine-grained token tracking with `TokenCounter`, supporting per-step and cumulative usage.
- Added `stepStartTime` and `taskStartTime` to Logger for step and task duration measurement.
- Enhanced logging outputs with token metrics and formatted duration display.
- Included final task statistics (steps, duration, token usage) in the `Done` log.
- Integrated token/timing tracking into agent workflows for improved execution reporting.

Remove outdated ADK optimization documents and internal development notes

- Deleted old ADK optimization roadmap, development notes, and working state files from `.serena/memories/` as they are no longer relevant and have been integrated into updated documentation.
- Consolidated key insights into `project-overview.md` for clarity and easier maintenance.

Remove outdated ADK optimization documents and internal development notes

- Deleted old ADK optimization roadmap, development notes, and working state files from `.serena/memories/` as they are no longer relevant and have been integrated into updated documentation.
- Consolidated key insights into `project-overview.md` for clarity and easier maintenance.

Remove outdated ADK optimization documents and internal development notes

- Deleted old ADK optimization roadmap, development notes, and working state files from `.serena/memories/` as they are no longer relevant and have been integrated into updated documentation.
- Consolidated key insights into `project-overview.md` for clarity and easier maintenance.

Add advanced browser automation tools and download capabilities to examples

- Introduced new `export/adktool.go` for browser automation, including `BrowserTool`, `MultiBrowserTool`, and related configurations.
- Added `browser/download.go` to handle file downloads with customizable configurations and improved reliability.
- Expanded examples with the `examples/adk_tool/main.go` showcasing research automation and `examples/download/main.go` demonstrating download capabilities.
- Enhanced integration with ADK agents, optimized prompts, and provided structured outputs for automation workflows.

Update .env file paths in examples for consistency

- Modified `.env` loading paths in `examples/scraping`, `examples/simple`, `examples/content_research`, `examples/research`, and `examples/multipage` to use the current directory instead of the project root (`../../.env`).

Remove deprecated test code and replace custom memory service with ADK-provided memory and artifact systems. Add advanced browser automation examples and introduce a multi-phase research workflow. Include ADK optimization roadmap and enhance agent configuration with persistent memory support.

Add Instagram lead generation example using browser automation

- Introduced `examples/ins/main.go`, demonstrating an Instagram lead generation workflow using `bua-go`.
- Configured agent with `gemini-3-pro-preview` model and added comprehensive prompts for qualified customer identification.
- Integrated real-time browser debugging, structured output, and error handling for robust execution.

Add comprehensive research automation example and prompt engineering best practices

- Introduced a multi-task research example under `examples/research/main.go`, demonstrating real-world applications of browser automation, such as multi-site navigation, data extraction, and comparative analysis.
- Added detailed prompts to ensure clarity, error handling, and structured outputs for complex workflows.
- Documented prompt engineering guidelines in a new file `.serena/memories/prompt-best-practices.md` to share best practices and reusable templates.
- Updated existing examples with richer objective-driven prompts and error-handling mechanisms.

Update to "gemini-3-flash-preview" model, add .env loading, and adjust viewport defaults

- Replaced "gemini-2.5-flash" with "gemini-3-flash-preview" model across all configurations and examples.
- Integrated `.env` file support for loading API keys using `godotenv`.
- Updated `DesktopViewport` to default to 1280×800 for improved responsiveness.
- Enhanced logging and annotations in examples with clearer output and explanations.

Introduce visual element annotation system and structured logging enhancements

- Added support for visual element annotations with configurable styles, colors, and overlays.
- Integrated a structured logging system with emoji indicators, step counting, and timestamps.
- Enhanced debugging capabilities with annotated screenshots and pre/post-action visualization.
- Updated examples and configuration to demonstrate the ShowAnnotations feature.
- Default model updated to "gemini-3-flash-preview" with increased input token limits.
Create LICENSE

commit.message:
Add development notes, enhance viewport handling, and provide multi-page automation example

- Introduced comprehensive `bua-go` development notes for architecture and usage guidance.
- Improved viewport responsiveness by aligning launcher window size and CDP emulator settings.
- Added a multi-page automation example showcasing Wikipedia, GitHub, and Go documentation workflows.
Fix JSONSchema tag formatting, update model references, and enhance session handling
Update examples to use the "gemini-3-pro-preview" model in agent configurations
Add .gitignore to exclude binaries, IDE files, OS files, and build artifacts
Update README with enhanced documentation and examples for bua-go usage
Add initial implementation with browser automation, ADK integration, and accessibility tree extraction logic
Initial commit

verification.payload:
tree 585432c04d790c76b6d744151da339072f34917e
author Tony An <anxuanzi@gmail.com> 1766890701 +0800
committer GitHub <noreply@github.com> 1766890701 +0800

Initial commit

readme_html:
Why BUA?

Traditional browser automation is fragile. CSS selectors break. XPaths change. Every website update means rewriting scripts.

BUA changes the game. Instead of writing brittle selectors, describe what you want in plain English:

result, _ := agent.Run(ctx, "Go to Amazon and find the best-rated wireless headphones under $100")

The AI agent sees the page, understands your intent, and adapts to any layout. No selectors. No maintenance. Just results.

✨ What Makes BUA Special

Feature Traditional Automation BUA
Selector Maintenance Constant updates needed Zero maintenance
Dynamic Content Complex waits & retries AI understands state
Multi-step Workflows Hundreds of lines One sentence
Layout Changes Scripts break Adapts automatically
New Sites Write new selectors Works immediately

🎯 Perfect For

  • Web Scraping - Extract data from any site without writing parsers
  • Form Automation - Fill applications, registrations, checkout flows
  • E2E Testing - Test user journeys with natural language
  • Data Entry - Automate repetitive web-based tasks
  • Research - Gather information across multiple sources
  • Monitoring - Track prices, inventory, content changes

🚀 Installation

go get github.com/anxuanzi/bua

Prerequisites

  • Go 1.25+
  • Chrome/Chromium installed on your system
  • Gemini API Key from Google AI Studio

⚡ Quick Start

package main

import (
	"context"
	"fmt"
	"log"
	"os"
	"time"

	"github.com/anxuanzi/bua"
)

func main() {
	// Create agent with your Gemini API key
	agent, err := bua.New(bua.Config{
		APIKey:   os.Getenv("GEMINI_API_KEY"),
		Headless: false, // Watch the magic happen
		Debug:    true,
	})
	if err != nil {
		log.Fatal(err)
	}
	defer agent.Close()

	ctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(context.Background(), 5*time.Minute)
	defer cancel()

	// Start the browser
	if err := agent.Start(ctx); err != nil {
		log.Fatal(err)
	}

	// Run a task with natural language
	result, err := agent.Run(ctx,
		"Go to Hacker News and find the top 3 stories about AI")
	if err != nil {
		log.Fatal(err)
	}

	fmt.Printf("✅ Success: %v\n", result.Success)
	fmt.Printf("📊 Steps taken: %d\n", len(result.Steps))
	fmt.Printf("⏱️  Duration: %v\n", result.Duration)
}

That's it. The agent navigates to Hacker News, scans the stories, identifies AI-related content, and returns the results.

🛠️ Features

🧠 Intelligent Navigation

BUA doesn't just click buttons—it understands web pages:

// The agent figures out HOW to accomplish the task
agent.Run(ctx, "Find flights from NYC to London for next weekend, sort by price")

// Multi-step workflows handled automatically
agent.Run(ctx, "Log into my account, go to settings, and change my timezone to PST")

👁️ Vision-Enabled

Screenshots are analyzed by the LLM for visual understanding:

cfg := bua.Config{
Preset: bua.PresetQuality, // High-res screenshots
// Vision is enabled by default
}

🥷 Stealth Mode

Built-in anti-detection measures help avoid bot blocking:

  • Navigator property spoofing
  • WebGL fingerprint masking
  • Plugin emulation
  • Human-like mouse movements
  • Random action delays

📸 Screenshot Annotations

Visual debugging with element indices overlaid on screenshots:

cfg := bua.Config{
ShowAnnotations: true, // See what the AI sees
ScreenshotDir:   "./debug",
}

🎛️ Flexible Presets

Optimize for speed, cost, or quality:

Preset Tokens Screenshot Best For
PresetFast 8K None (text-only) Simple tasks, lowest cost
PresetEfficient 16K 800px @ 60% Balanced cost/capability
PresetBalanced 32K 1280px @ 75% Default - most tasks
PresetQuality 64K 1920px @ 85% Complex visual tasks
PresetMax 128K 2560px @ 95% Maximum accuracy

🔐 Sensitive Data Protection

Automatic redaction of sensitive information in logs:

// API keys, passwords, SSNs, credit cards are automatically masked
// <secret type="api_key">[REDACTED]</secret>

🗂️ Tab Management

Handle complex multi-tab workflows:

// Open comparison shopping tabs
tab1, _ := agent.NewTab(ctx, "https://amazon.com")
tab2, _ := agent.NewTab(ctx, "https://ebay.com")

agent.SwitchTab(tab1)
agent.Run(ctx, "Search for 'mechanical keyboard'")

agent.SwitchTab(tab2)
agent.Run(ctx, "Search for 'mechanical keyboard' and compare prices")

💾 Session Persistence

Save and restore browser sessions:

cfg := bua.Config{
ProfileName: "my-shopping-session",
ProfileDir:  "~/.bua/profiles",
// Cookies, localStorage, login state preserved
}

⚙️ Configuration

Full Configuration Options

cfg := bua.Config{
// Required
APIKey: "your-gemini-api-key",

// LLM Settings
Model: "gemini-2.5-flash", // or "gemini-2.0-flash", etc.

// Browser Settings
Headless:    false,        // true for background operation
ProfileName: "persistent", // empty = temporary profile
ProfileDir:  "~/.bua/profiles",
Viewport:    &bua.Viewport{Width: 1920, Height: 1080},

// Agent Behavior
MaxSteps:    100, // Max actions before giving up
Preset:      bua.PresetBalanced,

// Screenshot Settings
ScreenshotDir:      "./screenshots",
ScreenshotMaxWidth: 1280,
ScreenshotQuality:  75,
TextOnly:           false, // true disables screenshots
ShowAnnotations:    false, // true shows element indices

// Visual Feedback
ShowHighlight:       true,
HighlightDurationMs: 300,

// Debugging
Debug: true,
}

Environment Variables

export GEMINI_API_KEY="your-api-key-here"

📖 Examples

Example 1: Web Research

result, _ := agent.Run(ctx, `
    Go to Wikipedia and find information about the Go programming language.
    Extract the release date, original author, and main features.
`)

fmt.Println(result.Data) // Extracted information

Example 2: E-commerce Automation

result, _ := agent.Run(ctx, `
    Go to Amazon, search for "USB-C hub", filter by 4+ stars,
    and find the cheapest option with Prime shipping.
`)

for _, step := range result.Steps {
fmt.Printf("[%d] %s: %s\n", step.Number, step.Action, step.NextGoal)
}

Example 3: Form Filling

result, _ := agent.Run(ctx, `
    Go to the contact form at example.com/contact.
    Fill in:
    - Name: John Doe
    - Email: john@example.com
    - Message: I'm interested in your services
    Then submit the form.
`)

Example 4: Multi-Page Workflow

// Navigate first
agent.Navigate(ctx, "https://github.com/login")

// Then automate
result, _ := agent.Run(ctx, `
    Log in with username 'myuser' and password from the password field.
    After logging in, go to my repositories and find the most starred one.
`)

🏗️ Architecture

┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                    Your Application                          │
├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│                   BUA Public API                             │
│         bua.New() → Start() → Run() → Close()               │
├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│                   Agent Layer                                │
│    ┌─────────────┐  ┌──────────────┐  ┌────────────────┐   │
│    │ BrowserAgent│  │ ADK Toolkit  │  │ Message Builder│   │
│    │  (LLM Loop) │  │ (20+ Tools)  │  │  (History)     │   │
│    └─────────────┘  └──────────────┘  └────────────────┘   │
├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│                   Browser Layer                              │
│    ┌─────────────┐  ┌──────────────┐  ┌────────────────┐   │
│    │   Browser   │  │    Page      │  │   Stealth      │   │
│    │ (Lifecycle) │  │ (Actions)    │  │   (Evasion)    │   │
│    └─────────────┘  └──────────────┘  └────────────────┘   │
├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│                   Support Layer                              │
│    ┌─────────────┐  ┌──────────────┐                        │
│    │     DOM     │  │  Screenshot  │                        │
│    │ (Extraction)│  │ (Annotation) │                        │
│    └─────────────┘  └──────────────┘                        │
├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│              go-rod (Chrome DevTools Protocol)              │
├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│                   Chrome / Chromium                          │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

Agent Loop

Task: "Search for Go tutorials"
           │
           ▼
┌──────────────────────┐
│   Get Page State     │◄─────────────────────┐
│   (DOM + Screenshot) │                      │
└──────────┬───────────┘                      │
           │                                  │
           ▼                                  │
┌──────────────────────┐                      │
│   LLM Reasoning      │                      │
│   (Gemini + Tools)   │                      │
└──────────┬───────────┘                      │
           │                                  │
           ▼                                  │
┌──────────────────────┐                      │
│   Execute Action     │                      │
│   (click, type, etc) │                      │
└──────────┬───────────┘                      │
           │                                  │
           ▼                                  │
    ┌──────────────┐         ┌───────────────┐
    │ Task Done?   │───No───►│ Update State  │
    └──────┬───────┘         └───────┬───────┘
           │Yes                      │
           ▼                         │
    ┌──────────────┐                 │
    │ Return Result│                 │
    └──────────────┘                 │
                                     └────────┘

🔧 Available Tools

The agent has access to 20+ browser automation tools:

Category Tools
Navigation navigate, go_back, go_forward, reload
Interaction click, type_text, clear_and_type, hover, double_click, focus
Scrolling scroll, scroll_to_element
Keyboard send_keys (Enter, Tab, Escape, etc.)
Observation get_page_state, screenshot, extract_content
JavaScript evaluate_js
Tabs new_tab, switch_tab, close_tab, list_tabs
Completion done

📊 Comparison with Browser-Use (Python)

BUA is inspired by the popular browser-use Python library. Here's how they compare:

Aspect Browser-Use (Python) BUA (Go)
Language Python 3.11+ Go 1.25+
LLM Support OpenAI, Claude, Gemini, Ollama Gemini (via ADK), other models soon.
Browser Engine Playwright go-rod (CDP direct)
Performance Good Excellent (compiled, no runtime)
Deployment Python environment Single binary
Memory Higher (Python + Node.js) Lower (native Go)
Concurrency asyncio Native goroutines
Anti-Detection ✅ ✅
Vision Support ✅ ✅
Custom Tools ✅ ✅

Why Choose BUA?

  • 🚀 Performance: Go's compiled nature means faster startup and lower memory
  • 📦 Simple Deployment: Single binary, no Python/Node.js dependencies
  • ⚡ Concurrency: Native goroutines for parallel operations
  • 🔒 Type Safety: Catch errors at compile time
  • 🏢 Enterprise Ready: Common choice for production services

🤝 Contributing

Contributions are welcome! Please feel free to submit a Pull Request.

  1. Fork the repository
  2. Create your feature branch (git checkout -b feature/amazing-feature)
  3. Commit your changes (git commit -m 'Add amazing feature')
  4. Push to the branch (git push origin feature/amazing-feature)
  5. Open a Pull Request
